pairs.question: What is the Georgian Basketball Super League and when was its first season?
pairs.answer: The Georgian Basketball Super League, also known as the Georgian Top League, is the highest professional basketball league in Georgia. The first season was played in 1991.

pairs.question: Which team won the inaugural season of the Georgian Basketball Super League?
pairs.answer: Dinamo Tbilisi won the inaugural season of the Georgian Basketball Super League in 1991.

pairs.question: Which team dominated the Georgian Basketball Super League in the 1990s and how many titles did they win?
pairs.answer: BC Vita Tbilisi dominated the Georgian Basketball Super League in the 1990s, winning the title a record 7 times.

pairs.question: How did the dominance of the Georgian Basketball Super League shift in the 2000s?
pairs.answer: In the 2000s, BC Batumi and later Energy Invest Rustavi dominated the Georgian Basketball Super League.

pairs.question: What notable change occurred in the 2013/14 season of the Georgian Basketball Super League regarding university teams?
pairs.answer: The 2013/14 season was the first when none of the country's universities were represented in the Superliga, following the Ministry of Education's decision to withdraw funding from professional sports teams.

pairs.question: Which teams dominated the Georgian Basketball Super League in the mid-2010s?
pairs.answer: In the mid-2010s, clubs attached to State departments dominated the Georgian Basketball Super League, with BC Armia (Ministry of Defense) and later BC MIA Academy (Ministry of Internal Affairs) winning titles.

pairs.question: What is the A-League (A-Liga) in Georgian basketball, and when was it introduced?
pairs.answer: The A-League (A-Liga) is the second tier of Georgian basketball, introduced in the 2014/15 season. It introduced relegation play-offs for teams at the bottom of the Superliga.

pairs.question: How did the relegation system for the Georgian Basketball Super League change starting from the 2015/16 season?
pairs.answer: Starting from the 2015/16 season, the club finishing last in the Georgian Basketball Super League is automatically relegated to the A-League.
